Journal.Title: Zhonghua wei chang wai ke za zhi = Chinese journal of gastrointestinal surgery
Journal.ISOAbbreviation: Zhonghua Wei Chang Wai Ke Za Zhi

Article.ArticleTitle: [Application value and implementation strategies of prehabilitation for perioperative gastrointestinal surgery patients].

Abstract.AbstractText: Surgical patients-particularly the elderly and those with gastrointestinal diseases, malignancies, or complex comorbidities-often present with multiple perioperative risk factors, such as malnutrition, diminished cardiopulmonary reserve, and psychological stress. These factors significantly compromise surgical tolerance and postoperative recovery. Prehabilitation, a preoperative optimization strategy grounded in the Enhanced Recovery After Surgery (ERAS) paradigm, aims to enhance patients' physiological and psychological reserves during the preoperative window through multimodal interventions. This approach is designed to improve surgical tolerance, minimize postoperative complications, and accelerate recovery. Prehabilitation typically integrates three core pillars: nutritional support to correct malnutrition and optimize metabolic status; individualized physical exercise to bolster cardiopulmonary function and muscle strength; and psychological intervention to alleviate anxiety, build confidence, and improve treatment adherence. Synthesizing recent domestic and international evidence with the authors' clinical experience, this article explores the value and implementation strategies of prehabilitation, with the aim of promoting its standardized application in surgical practice.

AffiliationInfo.Affiliation: Department of General Surgery, Gastrointestinal Surgery/Shanghai Clinical Nutrition Research Center, Zhongshan Hospital, Fudan University, Shanghai 200032, China.
Department of General Surgery, Gastrointestinal Surgery/Shanghai Clinical Nutrition Research Center, Zhongshan Hospital, Fudan University, Shanghai 200032, China.

Grant.Agency: National Natural Science Foundation of China

MedlineJournalInfo.MedlineTA: Zhonghua Wei Chang Wai Ke Za Zhi

OtherAbstract.AbstractText: 需要外科治疗的胃肠疾病、老年、肿瘤及复杂慢性病患者，常并存营养不良、心肺功能下降及心理应激等多重围手术期风险，严重影响手术耐受性及术后康复。预康复作为一种基于加速康复外科理念的术前优化策略，旨在通过手术前窗口期实施多模式干预，提升患者生理与心理储备，增强手术耐受力，减少术后并发症、加速术后康复。预康复通常整合三大支柱：科学的营养支持治疗以纠正营养不良、优化代谢；个体化的体能锻炼以增强心肺功能与肌肉力量；积极的心理干预以缓解焦虑、增强信心、提高治疗依从性。本文总结国内外最新研究结果，并结合笔者及其团队的实践经验，探讨胃肠外科患者预康复的价值及实施策略，以期促进预康复在外科中的进一步开展。.
